opencode-sse.el --- SSE event stream consumer for opencode.el -*- lexical-binding: t; -*-\n00002| \n00003| ;; Copyright (C) 2025 opencode.el contributors\n00004| ;; SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\n00005| \n00006| ;;; Commentary:\n00007| \n00008| ;; Server-Sent Events (SSE) consumer for the OpenCode event stream.\n00009| ;; Connects to GET /event (or /global/event), parses SSE format,\n00010| ;; and dispatches events to hook variables.\n00011| ;;\n00012| ;; SSE format:\n00013| ;;   event: <type>\n00014| ;;   data: <json>\n00015| ;;   id: <id>\n00016| ;;   (empty line = dispatch)\n00017| ;;\n00018| ;; Emacs 30: Benefits from `fast-read-process-output' for efficient\n00019| ;; process filter handling of the long-lived SSE connection.\n00020| \n00021| ;;; Code:\n00022| \n00023| (require 'json)\n00024| (require 'opencode-log)\n00025| (require 'opencode-util)\n00026| (require 'map)\n00027| \n00028| (declare-function opencode-server-url \"opencode-server\" (&optional path))\n00029| (declare-function opencode-server--connected-p \"opencode-server\" ())\n00030| \n00031| (defvar opencode-server-host)\n00032| (defvar opencode-server--port)\n00033| \n00034| (defgroup opencode-sse nil\n00035|   \"OpenCode SSE event stream.\"\n00036|   :group 'opencode\n00037|   :prefix \"opencode-sse-\")\n00038| \n00039| ;;; --- Customization ---\n00040| \n00041| (defcustom opencode-sse-heartbeat-timeout 60\n00042|   \"Seconds without any event before assuming connection lost.\n00043| OpenCode sends heartbeats every ~30s.\"\n00044|   :type 'integer\n00045|   :group 'opencode-sse)\n00046| \n00047| (defcustom opencode-sse-max-reconnect-delay 30\n00048|   \"Maximum seconds between reconnect attempts.\"\n00049|   :type 'integer\n00050|   :group 'opencode-sse)\n00051| \n00052| (defcustom opencode-sse-auto-reconnect t\n00053|   \"When non-nil, automatically reconnect on connection loss.\"\n00054|   :type 'boolean\n00055|   :group 'opencode-sse)\n00056| \n00057| ;;; --- Hook variables ---\n00058| \n00059| (defvar opencode-sse-event-hook nil\n00060|   \"Hook run for ALL SSE events.\n00061| Each function receives one argument: the event plist\n00062| with :type, :properties, and optionally :directory.\")\n00063| \n00064| (defvar opencode-sse-server-connected-hook nil\n00065|   \"Hook run when `server.connected' event is received.\")\n00066| \n00067| (defvar opencode-sse-server-heartbeat-hook nil\n00068|   \"Hook run when `server.heartbeat' event is received.\")\n00069| \n00070| (defvar opencode-sse-session-updated-hook nil\n00071|   \"Hook run when `session.updated' event is received.\n00072| Functions receive the event plist with :properties containing :sessionID.\")\n00073| \n00074| (defvar opencode-sse-message-updated-hook nil\n00075|   \"Hook run when `message.updated' event is received.\n00076| Functions receive the event plist.\")\n00077| \n00078| (defvar opencode-sse-message-part-updated-hook nil\n00079|   \"Hook run when `message.part.updated' event is received.\n00080| This is the core streaming event.  Functions receive the event plist\n00081| with :properties containing :sessionID, :messageID, and :part.\")\n00082| \n00083| (defvar opencode-sse-message-part-removed-hook nil\n00084|   \"Hook run when `message.part.removed' event is received.\")\n00085| \n00086| (defvar opencode-sse-message-removed-hook nil\n00087|   \"Hook run when `message.removed' event is received.\")\n00088| \n00089| (defvar opencode-sse-session-status-hook nil\n00090|   \"Hook run when `session.status' event is received.\n00091| Functions receive event plist with :sessionID and :status.\")\n00092| \n00093| (defvar opencode-sse-session-idle-hook nil\n00094|   \"Hook run when `session.idle' event is received.\n00095| Functions receive the event plist with :properties containing :sessionID.\")\n00096| \n00097| (defvar opencode-sse-session-diff-hook nil\n00098|   \"Hook run when `session.diff' event is received.\")\n00099| \n00100| (defvar opencode-sse-question-asked-hook nil\n00101|   \"Hook run when `question.asked' event is received.\")\n00102| \n00103| (defvar opencode-sse-permission-asked-hook nil\n00104|   \"Hook run when `permission.asked' event is received.\")\n00105| \n00106| (defvar opencode-sse-global-disposed-hook nil\n00107|   \"Hook run when `global.disposed' event is received.\")\n00108| \n00109| (defvar opencode-sse-server-instance-disposed-hook nil\n00110|   \"Hook run when `server.instance.disposed' event is received.\n00111| The server disposes instances when files change or the project reloads.\n00112| This is informational only — the global SSE connection remains alive.\")\n00113| \n00114| (defvar opencode-sse-session-deleted-hook nil\n00115|   \"Hook run when `session.deleted' event is received.\")\n00116| \n00117| (defvar opencode-sse-session-error-hook nil\n00118|   \"Hook run when `session.error' event is received.\")\n00119| \n00120| (defvar opencode-sse-session-compacted-hook nil\n00121|   \"Hook run when `session.compacted' event is received.\")\n00122| \n00123| (defvar opencode-sse-session-created-hook nil\n00124|   \"Hook run when `session.created' event is received.\")\n00125| \n00126| (defvar opencode-sse-todo-updated-hook nil\n00127|   \"Hook run when `todo.updated' event is received.\")\n00128| \n00129| (defvar opencode-sse-permission-replied-hook nil\n00130|   \"Hook run when `permission.replied' event is received.\")\n00131| \n00132| (defvar opencode-sse-question-replied-hook nil\n00133|   \"Hook run when `question.replied' event is received.\")\n00134| \n00135| (defvar opencode-sse-question-rejected-hook nil\n00136|   \"Hook run when `question.rejected' event is received.\")\n00137| \n00138| (defvar opencode-sse-installation-update-available-hook nil\n00139|   \"Hook run when `installation.update-available' event is received.\")\n00140| ;;; --- Internal state ---\n00141| \n00142| (defvar opencode-sse--process nil\n00143|   \"The network process for the SSE connection.\")\n00144| \n00145| (defvar opencode-sse--buffer nil\n00146|   \"Internal buffer for accumulating partial SSE data.\n00147| Uses an Emacs buffer (gap buffer) instead of string concatenation\n00148| for O(1) append and efficient line-by-line scanning.\")\n00149| \n00150| (defvar opencode-sse--current-event nil\n00151|   \"Plist for the SSE event currently being built.\n00152| Keys: :event-type, :data, :id.\")\n00153| \n00154| (defvar opencode-sse--last-event-id nil\n00155|   \"The ID of the last received SSE event.\")\n00156| \n00157| (defvar opencode-sse--reconnect-timer nil\n00158|   \"Timer for reconnect attempts.\")\n00159| \n00160| (defvar opencode-sse--reconnect-delay 1\n00161|   \"Current reconnect delay in seconds (exponential backoff).\")\n00162| \n00163| (defvar opencode-sse--heartbeat-timer nil\n00164|   \"Timer for heartbeat monitoring.\")\n00165| \n00166| (defvar opencode-sse--last-event-time nil\n00167|   \"Timestamp of the last received event (float-time).\")\n00168| \n00169| (defvar opencode-sse--url nil\n00170|   \"The SSE endpoint URL, stored for reconnection.\")\n00171| \n00172| (defvar opencode-sse--response-buffer nil\n00173|   \"The url.el response buffer for the SSE connection.\")\n00174| \n00175| ;;; --- Predicates ---\n00176| \n00177| (defun opencode-sse--connected-p ()\n00178|   \"Return non-nil if the SSE stream is connected.\"\n00179|   (and opencode-sse--process\n00180|        (process-live-p opencode-sse--process)))\n00181| \n00182| (defun opencode-sse-connected-p ()\n00183|   \"Return non-nil if the SSE stream is connected.\n00184| Public API.\"\n00185|   (opencode-sse--connected-p))\n00186| \n00187| ;;; --- State management ---\n00188| \n00189| (defun opencode-sse--ensure-buffer ()\n00190|   \"Ensure the SSE accumulation buffer exists and return it.\"\n00191|   (or (and opencode-sse--buffer\n00192|            (buffer-live-p opencode-sse--buffer)\n00193|            opencode-sse--buffer)\n00194|       (setq opencode-sse--buffer\n00195|             (let ((buf (generate-new-buffer \" *opencode-sse-accum*\")))\n00196|               (with-current-buffer buf\n00197|                 (set-buffer-multibyte t))\n00198|               buf))))\n00199| \n00200| (defun opencode-sse--kill-buffer ()\n00201|   \"Kill the SSE accumulation buffer if it exists.\"\n00202|   (when (and opencode-sse--buffer (buffer-live-p opencode-sse--buffer))\n00203|     (kill-buffer opencode-sse--buffer))\n00204|   (setq opencode-sse--buffer nil))\n00205| \n00206| (defun opencode-sse--reset-state ()\n00207|   \"Reset all SSE internal state.\"\n00208|   (opencode-sse--kill-buffer)\n00209|   (setq opencode-sse--current-event nil\n00210|         opencode-sse--last-event-time nil))\n00211| \n00212| ;;; --- SSE line parser ---\n00213| \n00214| (defun opencode-sse--process-line (line)\n00215|   \"Process a single SSE LINE.\n00216| Accumulates event data and dispatches on empty lines.\"\n00217|   (cond\n00218|    ;; Empty line → dispatch accumulated event\n00219|    ((string-empty-p line)\n00220|     (when opencode-sse--current-event\n00221|       (let ((event-type (or (plist-get opencode-sse--current-event :event-type)\n00222|                             \"message\"))\n00223|             (data (plist-get opencode-sse--current-event :data))\n00224|             (id (plist-get opencode-sse--current-event :id)))\n00225|         (when data\n00226|           (opencode-sse--dispatch-event event-type data id)))\n00227|       (setq opencode-sse--current-event nil)))\n00228|    ;; Comment line (starts with :)\n00229|    ((string-prefix-p \":\" line)\n00230|     nil)\n00231|    ;; event: <type>\n00232|    ((string-match \"^event: ?\\\\(.*\\\\)\" line)\n00233|     (setq opencode-sse--current-event\n00234|           (plist-put (or opencode-sse--current-event '())\n00235|                      :event-type (match-string 1 line))))\n00236|    ;; data: <json>\n00237|    ((string-match \"^data: ?\\\\(.*\\\\)\" line)\n00238|     (let ((existing-data (plist-get opencode-sse--current-event :data)))\n00239|       (setq opencode-sse--current-event\n00240|             (plist-put (or opencode-sse--current-event '()) :data\n00241|                        (if existing-data\n00242|                            (concat existing-data \"\\n\" (match-string 1 line))\n00243|                          (match-string 1 line))))))\n00244|    ;; id: <id>\n00245|    ((string-match \"^id: ?\\\\(.*\\\\)\" line)\n00246|     (setq opencode-sse--current-event\n00247|           (plist-put (or opencode-sse--current-event '())\n00248|                      :id (match-string 1 line)))\n00249|     (setq opencode-sse--last-event-id (match-string 1 line)))\n00250|    ;; Unknown field — ignore per spec\n00251|    (t nil)))\n00252| \n00253| ;;; --- Event dispatch ---\n00254| \n00255| (defun opencode-sse--dispatch-event (event-type data-string &optional id)\n00256|   \"Dispatch an SSE event.\n00257| EVENT-TYPE is the event type string.\n00258| DATA-STRING is the raw data (JSON string).\n00259| ID is the optional event ID.\"\n00260|   (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: raw event: type=%s data=%s\"\n00261|            event-type\n00262|            data-string)\n00263|   (setq opencode-sse--last-event-time (float-time))\n00264|   ;; Reset reconnect backoff on successful event\n00265|   (setq opencode-sse--reconnect-delay 1)\n00266|   (condition-case err\n00267|       (let* ((json-data (opencode--json-parse data-string))\n00268|              ;; Build the event plist for hooks.\n00269|              ;; Global events have {directory, payload: {type, properties}}.\n00270|              ;; Instance events have {type, properties} directly.\n00271|              (event (cond\n00272|                      ;; Global event format\n00273|                      ((plist-get json-data :payload)\n00274|                       (let ((payload (plist-get json-data :payload)))\n00275|                         (list :type (or (plist-get payload :type) event-type)\n00276|                               :properties (plist-get payload :properties)\n00277|                               :directory (plist-get json-data :directory)\n00278|                               :id id)))\n00279|                      ;; Instance event format\n00280|                      ((plist-get json-data :type)\n00281|                       (list :type (plist-get json-data :type)\n00282|                             :properties (plist-get json-data :properties)\n00283|                             :id id))\n00284|                      ;; Fallback: use the SSE event-type field\n00285|                      (t (list :type event-type\n00286|                               :properties json-data\n00287|                               :id id))))\n00288|              (type (plist-get event :type)))\n00289|         (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: [%s] dir=%s props-keys=%S\"\n00290|                  type\n00291|                  (plist-get event :directory)\n00292|                  (and (plist-get event :properties)\n00293|                       (map-keys (plist-get event :properties))))\n00294|         ;; Run catch-all hook\n00295|         (run-hook-with-args 'opencode-sse-event-hook event)\n00296|         ;; Run type-specific hook\n00297|         (when-let* ((hook (opencode-sse--hook-for-type type)))\n00298|           (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: dispatching to %s (%d listeners)\"\n00299|                    hook (length (symbol-value hook)))\n00300|           (run-hook-with-args hook event)))\n00301|     (error\n00302|      (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: Error dispatching event %s: %s\"\n00303|               event-type (error-message-string err)))))\n00304| \n00305| (defun opencode-sse--hook-for-type (type)\n00306|   \"Return the hook variable for event TYPE string, or nil.\"\n00307|   (cond\n00308|    ((string= type \"server.connected\")       'opencode-sse-server-connected-hook)\n00309|    ((string= type \"server.heartbeat\")       'opencode-sse-server-heartbeat-hook)\n00310|    ((string= type \"session.updated\")        'opencode-sse-session-updated-hook)\n00311|    ((string= type \"message.updated\")        'opencode-sse-message-updated-hook)\n00312|    ((string= type \"message.part.updated\")   'opencode-sse-message-part-updated-hook)\n00313|    ((string= type \"message.part.removed\")   'opencode-sse-message-part-removed-hook)\n00314|    ((string= type \"message.removed\")        'opencode-sse-message-removed-hook)\n00315|    ((string= type \"session.status\")         'opencode-sse-session-status-hook)\n00316|    ((string= type \"session.idle\")           'opencode-sse-session-idle-hook)\n00317|    ((string= type \"session.diff\")           'opencode-sse-session-diff-hook)\n00318|    ((string= type \"question.asked\")         'opencode-sse-question-asked-hook)\n00319|    ((string= type \"permission.asked\")       'opencode-sse-permission-asked-hook)\n00320|    ((string= type \"global.disposed\")        'opencode-sse-global-disposed-hook)\n00321|    ((string= type \"server.instance.disposed\") 'opencode-sse-server-instance-disposed-hook)\n00322|    ((string= type \"session.deleted\")       'opencode-sse-session-deleted-hook)\n00323|    ((string= type \"session.error\")         'opencode-sse-session-error-hook)\n00324|    ((string= type \"session.compacted\")     'opencode-sse-session-compacted-hook)\n00325|    ((string= type \"session.created\")       'opencode-sse-session-created-hook)\n00326|    ((string= type \"todo.updated\")          'opencode-sse-todo-updated-hook)\n00327|    ((string= type \"permission.replied\")    'opencode-sse-permission-replied-hook)\n00328|    ((string= type \"question.replied\")      'opencode-sse-question-replied-hook)\n00329|    ((string= type \"question.rejected\")     'opencode-sse-question-rejected-hook)\n00330|    ((string= type \"installation.update-available\") 'opencode-sse-installation-update-available-hook)\n00331|    (t nil)))\n00332| \n00333| ;;; --- Process filter ---\n00334| \n00335| (defun opencode-sse--filter (_process output)\n00336|   \"Process filter for the SSE connection.\n00337| _PROCESS is the network process.  OUTPUT is new data received.\n00338| Uses an internal Emacs buffer as accumulator for O(1) append and\n00339| efficient line-by-line scanning, avoiding O(n^2) string concat.\"\n00340|   (let ((accum-buf (opencode-sse--ensure-buffer)))\n00341|     (with-current-buffer accum-buf\n00342|       ;; O(1) append — gap buffer moves gap to end\n00343|       (goto-char (point-max))\n00344|       (insert output)\n00345|       ;; Scan complete lines from the beginning.\n00346|       ;; After each line is processed, delete it from the buffer so\n00347|       ;; point-min always marks the start of the next (or partial) line.\n00348|       (goto-char (point-min))\n00349|       (let ((line-count 0))\n00350|         (while (search-forward \"\\n\" nil t)\n00351|           (let* ((nl-pos (point))\n00352|                  (line-end (1- nl-pos))\n00353|                  (raw-line (buffer-substring-no-properties\n00354|                             (point-min) line-end))\n00355|                  (line (if (and (> (length raw-line) 0)\n00356|                                (eq (aref raw-line (1- (length raw-line))) ?\\r))\n00357|                            (substring raw-line 0 -1)\n00358|                          raw-line)))\n00359|             ;; Delete consumed line including its newline\n00360|             (delete-region (point-min) nl-pos)\n00361|             ;; Point is now at new point-min; next search-forward\n00362|             ;; will scan from here.\n00363|             (goto-char (point-min))\n00364|             (setq line-count (1+ line-count))\n00365|             (opencode-sse--process-line line)))\n00366|         (when (> line-count 0)\n00367|           (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: processed %d lines, %d bytes remaining\"\n00368|                            line-count (- (point-max) (point-min))))))))\n00369| \n00370| ;;; --- Sentinel ---\n00371| \n00372| (defun opencode-sse--sentinel (_process event)\n00373|   \"Sentinel for the SSE connection process.\n00374| Handles disconnection and triggers reconnect.\"\n00375|   (let ((event-str (string-trim event)))\n00376|     (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: sentinel event: %s\" event-str)\n00377|     (unless (string-match-p \"\\\\`open\" event-str)\n00378|       ;; Connection lost\n00379|       (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: connection lost, will reconnect=%s\" opencode-sse-auto-reconnect)\n00380|       (setq opencode-sse--process nil)\n00381|       (opencode-sse--stop-heartbeat)\n00382|       (when opencode-sse-auto-reconnect\n00383|         (opencode-sse--schedule-reconnect)))))\n00384| \n00385| ;;; --- Connect / Disconnect ---\n00386| \n00387| (defun opencode-sse-connect (&optional _url)\n00388|   \"Connect to the SSE event stream via curl subprocess.\n00389| Uses `curl --no-buffer' for true streaming (url.el buffers responses).\n00390| Connects to /global/event which does NOT need X-OpenCode-Directory.\"\n00391|   (when (opencode-sse-connected-p)\n00392|     (opencode-sse-disconnect))\n00393|   (opencode-sse--reset-state)\n00394|   (let* ((process-adaptive-read-buffering nil)\n00395|          (host opencode-server-host)\n00396|          (port opencode-server--port)\n00397|          (sse-url (format \"http://%s:%d/global/event\" host port))\n00398|          (curl-args (list \"-s\" \"-N\"\n00399|                           \"-H\" \"Accept: text/event-stream\"\n00400|                           \"-H\" \"Cache-Control: no-cache\"\n00401|                           sse-url)))\n00402|     (setq opencode-sse--url sse-url)\n00403|     (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: connecting via curl to %s\" sse-url)\n00404|     (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: curl args: %S\" curl-args)\n00405|     (let ((proc (apply #'start-process\n00406|                        \"opencode-sse\" nil\n00407|                        (opencode-sse--curl-path) curl-args)))\n00408|       (when proc\n00409|         (setq opencode-sse--process proc)\n00410|         (set-process-filter proc #'opencode-sse--filter)\n00411|         (set-process-sentinel proc #'opencode-sse--sentinel)\n00412|         (set-process-query-on-exit-flag proc nil)\n00413|         (set-process-coding-system proc 'utf-8 'utf-8)\n00414|         ;; Disable adaptive read buffering for the SSE process.\n00415|         ;; The default (t) makes Emacs coalesce small reads with\n00416|         ;; increasing delay, which destroys streaming latency —\n00417|         ;; deltas arrive seconds to tens of seconds late after a\n00418|         ;; long session.\n00419|         (when (fboundp 'set-process-adaptive-read-buffering)\n00420|           (set-process-adaptive-read-buffering proc nil))\n00421|         (opencode-sse--start-heartbeat)\n00422|         (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: curl process started, pid=%s\"\n00423|                  (process-id proc))))))\n00424| \n00425| (defun opencode-sse-disconnect ()\n00426|   \"Disconnect from the SSE event stream.\"\n00427|   (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: disconnecting\")\n00428|   (when opencode-sse--reconnect-timer\n00429|     (cancel-timer opencode-sse--reconnect-timer)\n00430|     (setq opencode-sse--reconnect-timer nil))\n00431|   (opencode-sse--stop-heartbeat)\n00432|   (when (and opencode-sse--process (process-live-p opencode-sse--process))\n00433|     (set-process-sentinel opencode-sse--process #'ignore)\n00434|     (delete-process opencode-sse--process))\n00435|   (when (and opencode-sse--response-buffer\n00436|              (buffer-live-p opencode-sse--response-buffer))\n00437|     (kill-buffer opencode-sse--response-buffer))\n00438|   (setq opencode-sse--process nil\n00439|         opencode-sse--response-buffer nil)\n00440|   (opencode-sse--reset-state))\n00441| \n00442| ;;; --- Reconnect ---\n00443| \n00444| (defun opencode-sse--schedule-reconnect ()\n00445|   \"Schedule a reconnect attempt with exponential backoff.\"\n00446|   (when opencode-sse--reconnect-timer\n00447|     (cancel-timer opencode-sse--reconnect-timer))\n00448|   (setq opencode-sse--reconnect-timer\n00449|         (run-with-timer opencode-sse--reconnect-delay nil\n00450|                         #'opencode-sse--do-reconnect))\n00451|   ;; Increase backoff\n00452|   (setq opencode-sse--reconnect-delay\n00453|         (min (* opencode-sse--reconnect-delay 2)\n00454|              opencode-sse-max-reconnect-delay)))\n00455| \n00456| (defun opencode-sse--do-reconnect ()\n00457|   \"Attempt to reconnect to the SSE stream.\n00458| Only reconnects if the server is still connected.\"\n00459|   (setq opencode-sse--reconnect-timer nil)\n00460|   (condition-case err\n00461|       (when (and opencode-sse--url\n00462|                  (opencode-server--connected-p))\n00463|         (opencode-sse--connect))\n00464|     (error\n00465|      (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: Reconnect failed: %s\" (error-message-string err))\n00466|      (when opencode-sse-auto-reconnect\n00467|        (opencode-sse--schedule-reconnect)))))\n00468| \n00469| ;;; --- Heartbeat monitoring ---\n00470| \n00471| (defun opencode-sse--start-heartbeat ()\n00472|   \"Start heartbeat monitoring.\"\n00473|   (opencode-sse--stop-heartbeat)\n00474|   (setq opencode-sse--last-event-time (float-time))\n00475|   (setq opencode-sse--heartbeat-timer\n00476|         (run-with-timer opencode-sse-heartbeat-timeout\n00477|                         opencode-sse-heartbeat-timeout\n00478|                         #'opencode-sse--check-heartbeat)))\n00479| \n00480| (defun opencode-sse--stop-heartbeat ()\n00481|   \"Stop heartbeat monitoring.\"\n00482|   (when opencode-sse--heartbeat-timer\n00483|     (cancel-timer opencode-sse--heartbeat-timer)\n00484|     (setq opencode-sse--heartbeat-timer nil)))\n00485| \n00486| (defun opencode-sse--check-heartbeat ()\n00487|   \"Check if we've received events within the heartbeat timeout.\"\n00488|   (when (and opencode-sse--last-event-time\n00489|              (> (- (float-time) opencode-sse--last-event-time)\n00490|                 opencode-sse-heartbeat-timeout))\n00491|     ;; Heartbeat timeout — assume connection lost\n00492|     (opencode--debug \"opencode-sse: Heartbeat timeout, reconnecting...\")\n00493|     (opencode-sse-disconnect)\n00494|     (when opencode-sse-auto-reconnect\n00495|       (opencode-sse--schedule-reconnect))))\n00496| \n00497| ;;; --- Curl transport ---\n00498| \n00499| (defvar opencode-sse--curl-path-cache nil\n00500|   \"Cached path to curl executable.\")\n00501| \n00502| (defun opencode-sse--curl-path ()\n00503|   \"Return the path to curl, caching the result.\"\n00504|   (or opencode-sse--curl-path-cache\n00505|       (setq opencode-sse--curl-path-cache\n00506|             (or (executable-find \"curl\")\n00507|                 (error \"curl not found in PATH\")))))\n00508| \n00509| ;;; --- Internal connect/disconnect ---\n00510| \n00511| (defun opencode-sse--connect ()\n00512|   \"Internal connect function used by reconnect logic.\"\n00513|   (opencode-sse-connect))\n00514| \n00515| (defun opencode-sse--disconnect ()\n00516|   \"Internal disconnect — same as `opencode-sse-disconnect'.\"\n00517|   (opencode-sse-disconnect))\n00518| \n00519| (provide 'opencode-sse)\n00520| ;;; opencode-sse.el ends here\n00521| \n\n(End of file - total 521 lines)\n</file>\n\n[Project README: /Users/bytedance/Documents/opencode.el/README.md]\n","title":"opencode-sse.el","metadata":{"preview":";;; opencode-sse.el --- SSE event stream consumer for opencode.el -*- lexical-binding: t; -*-\n\n;; Copyright (C) 2025 opencode.el contributors\n;; SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\n\n;;; Commentary:\n\n;; Server-Sent Events (SSE) consumer for the OpenCode event stream.\n;; Connects to GET /event (or /global/event), parses SSE format,\n;; and dispatches events to hook variables.\n;;\n;; SSE format:\n;;   event: <type>\n;;   data: <json>\n;;   id: <id>\n;;   (empty line = dispatch)\n;;\n;; Emacs 30: Benefits from `fast-read-process-output' for efficient\n;; process filter handling of the long-lived SSE connection.\n","truncated":false},"time":{"start":1772839851533,"end":1772839853076}}}}}}
# [07:30:53.086] chat: on-part-updated part=prt_cc57d51cf001PGQKDcXl5TASjQ type=tool delta=nil
# [07:30:53.097] message.part.updated
:sse {"directory":"/Users/bytedance/Documents/opencode.el","payload":{"type":"message.part.updated","properties":{"part":{"id":"prt_cc57d5465001HpXOgfSva3Vnw0","sessionID":"ses_33d619ed8ffeM7IJNc5B58iJO7","messageID":"msg_cc57ce428001f2o6eeX2xhAIjd","type":"tool","callID":"toolu_vrtx_014rr4eZ7bzbXHXJKTsGBAD2","tool":"read","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/bytedance/Documents/opencode.el/opencode-server.el"},"output":"<file>\n00001| ;;; opencode-server.el --- Server subprocess lifecycle for opencode.el -*- lexical-binding: t; -*-\n00002| \n00003| ;; Copyright (C) 2025 opencode.el contributors\n00004| ;; SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\n00005| \n00006| ;;; Commentary:\n00007| \n00008| ;; Manages the OpenCode server subprocess lifecycle:\n00009| ;; - Spawn `opencode serve --port 0` and parse the dynamically assigned port\n00010| ;; - Process sentinel for crash detection and auto-restart\n00011| ;; - Health check with retry and exponential backoff\n00012| ;; - Graceful shutdown via POST /global/dispose\n00013| ;; - Connect mode for attaching to an existing server\n00014| ;; - Per-directory server tracking\n00015| ;;\n00016| ;; Emacs 30: Benefits from `fast-read-process-output' (native default\n00017| ;; process filter) for efficient subprocess stdout/stderr handling.\n00018| ;;\n00019| ;; WHY HTTP IS REQUIRED:\n00020| ;; The TUI avoids HTTP by running Server.App() (Hono) in a Bun Worker thread\n00021| ;; and calling .fetch() directly via inter-thread RPC.  This is a Bun-specific\n00022| ;; optimization that Emacs cannot replicate — Emacs has no JavaScript runtime.\n00023| ;; We must use real HTTP, which matches how `opencode attach` and the Web App\n00024| ;; work.  See AGENTS.md \"Why Emacs Requires HTTP\" for details.\n00025| \n00026| ;;; Code:\n00027| \n00028| (require 'url)\n00029| (require 'url-http)\n00030| (require 'opencode-util)\n00031| \n00032| (defvar url-http-end-of-headers)\n00033| \n00034| (defgroup opencode-server nil\n00035|   \"OpenCode server subprocess management.\"\n00036|   :group 'opencode\n00037|   :prefix \"opencode-server-\")\n00038| \n00039| ;;; --- Customization ---\n00040| \n00041| (defcustom opencode-server-command \"opencode\"\n00042|   \"Path to the opencode executable.\"\n00043|   :type 'string\n00044|   :group 'opencode-server)\n00045| \n00046| (defcustom opencode-server-args '(\"serve\" \"--port\" \"0\" \"--print-logs\")\n00047|   \"Arguments passed to the opencode server command.\n00048| \\\"--port 0\\\" makes the OS assign a free port.\"\n00049|   :type '(repeat string)\n00050|   :group 'opencode-server)\n00051| \n00052| (defcustom opencode-server-log-level \"DEBUG\"\n00053|   \"Log level for the opencode server subprocess.\"\n00054|   :type '(choice (const \"DEBUG\") (const \"INFO\") (const \"WARN\") (const \"ERROR\"))\n00055|   :group 'opencode-server)\n00056| \n00057| (defcustom opencode-server-auto-restart t\n00058|   \"When non-nil, automatically restart the server if it crashes.\"\n00059|   :type 'boolean\n00060|   :group 'opencode-server)\n00061| \n00062| (defcustom opencode-server-host \"127.0.0.1\"\n00063|   \"Hostname for the opencode server.\"\n00064|   :type 'string\n00065|   :group 'opencode-server)\n00066| \n00067| (defcustom opencode-server-port nil\n00068|   \"Fixed port for the opencode server.\n00069| When nil (default), uses --port 0 for auto-assignment.\n00070| Set to a number to connect to an existing server (no subprocess spawned).\"\n00071|   :type '(choice (const :tag \"Auto-assign (spawn server)\" nil)\n00072|                  (integer :tag \"Fixed port (connect to existing)\"))\n00073|   :group 'opencode-server)\n00074| \n00075| (defcustom opencode-server-health-retries 5\n00076|   \"Number of health check retry attempts on startup.\"\n00077|   :type 'integer\n00078|   :group 'opencode-server)\n00079| \n00080| (defcustom opencode-server-restart-delay 2\n00081|   \"Seconds to wait before auto-restarting a crashed server.\"\n00082|   :type 'number\n00083|   :group 'opencode-server)\n00084| \n00085| ;;; --- Internal state ---\n00086| \n00087| (defvar opencode-server--process nil\n00088|   \"The opencode server process object, or nil if not running.\")\n00089| \n00090| (defvar opencode-server--port nil\n00091|   \"The port the server is listening on, or nil if not connected.\")\n00092| \n00093| (defvar opencode-server--status nil\n00094|   \"Server connection status.\n00095| One of: nil, `starting', `connected', `disconnected', `error'.\")\n00096| \n00097| (defvar opencode-server--stdout-buffer nil\n00098|   \"Internal buffer for accumulating partial stdout from the server process.\n00099| Uses an Emacs buffer (gap buffer) instead of string concatenation\n00100| for O(1) append and efficient line-by-line scanning.\")\n00101| \n00102| (defvar opencode-server--restart-timer nil\n00103|   \"Timer for auto-restart after crash.\")\n00104| \n00105| (defvar opencode-server--log-buffer-name \"*opencode: log*\"\n00106|   \"Name of the server log buffer.\")\n00107| \n00108| (defvar opencode-server--port-callback nil\n00109|   \"Callback function invoked when port is parsed from server stdout.\n00110| Called with one argument: the port number.\")\n00111| \n00112| (defvar opencode-server--managed-p nil\n00113|   \"Non-nil when we spawned the server (managed mode).\n00114| Nil when connecting to an existing server (connect mode).\")\n00115| \n00116| (defcustom opencode-server-log-max-lines 5000\n00117|   \"Maximum number of lines to keep in the server log buffer.\n00118| When exceeded, the oldest lines are deleted.  Set to nil to disable truncation.\"\n00119|   :type '(choice (integer :tag \"Max lines\")\n00120|                  (const :tag \"Unlimited\" nil))\n00121|   :group 'opencode-server)\n00122| \n00123| ;;; --- Logging ---\n00124| \n00125| (defvar opencode-server--log-line-count 0\n00126|   \"Approximate number of lines in the server log buffer.\n00127| Tracked incrementally to avoid O(n) scanning on every log call.\")\n00128| \n00129| (defun opencode-server--log (format-string &rest args)\n00130|   \"Log a message to the opencode log buffer.\n00131| FORMAT-STRING and ARGS are passed to `format'.\n00132| Automatically truncates to `opencode-server-log-max-lines'.\"\n00133|   (let ((buf (get-buffer-create opencode-server--log-buffer-name))\n00134|         (msg (apply #'format format-string args))\n00135|         (timestamp (format-time-string \"%H:%M:%S\")))\n00136|     (with-current-buffer buf\n00137|       (goto-char (point-max))\n00138|       (let ((inhibit-read-only t))\n00139|         (insert (format \"[%s] %s\\n\" timestamp msg)))\n00140|       (setq opencode-server--log-line-count\n00141|             (1+ opencode-server--log-line-count))\n00142|       ;; Truncate: delete half at once to amortize cost\n00143|       (when (and opencode-server-log-max-lines\n00144|                  (> opencode-server--log-line-count\n00145|                     opencode-server-log-max-lines))\n00146|         (let ((delete-count (/ opencode-server-log-max-lines 2)))\n00147|           (save-excursion\n00148|             (goto-char (point-min))\n00149|             (forward-line delete-count)\n00150|             (let ((inhibit-read-only t))\n00151|               (delete-region (point-min) (point))))\n00152|           (setq opencode-server--log-line-count\n00153|                 (- opencode-server--log-line-count delete-count)))))))\n00154| \n00155| ;;; --- URL construction ---\n00156| \n00157| (defun opencode-server--url (&optional path)\n00158|   \"Return the base URL for the OpenCode server, optionally with PATH appended.\n00159| Does NOT call `opencode-server-ensure' — for internal use.\n00160| Signals `user-error' if not connected.\"\n00161|   (unless opencode-server--port\n00162|     (user-error \"OpenCode server not connected (no port)\"))\n00163|   (unless (eq opencode-server--status 'connected)\n00164|     (user-error \"OpenCode server status is `%s', expected `connected'\"\n00165|                 opencode-server--status))\n00166|   (let ((base (format \"http://%s:%d\" opencode-server-host opencode-server--port)))\n00167|     (if path\n00168|         (concat base (if (string-prefix-p \"/\" path) path (concat \"/\" path)))\n00169|       base)))\n00170| \n00171| (defun opencode-server-url (&optional path)\n00172|   \"Return the base URL for the OpenCode server, optionally with PATH appended.\n00173| Signals an error if the server is not connected.\"\n00174|   (opencode-server-ensure)\n00175|   (opencode-server--url path))\n00176| \n00177| ;;; --- Predicates ---\n00178| \n00179| (defun opencode-server--connected-p ()\n00180|   \"Return non-nil if the server is connected and ready.\"\n00181|   (and opencode-server--port\n00182|        (eq opencode-server--status 'connected)))\n00183| \n00184| (defun opencode-server-connected-p ()\n00185|   \"Return non-nil if the server is connected and ready.\n00186| Public API — same as `opencode-server--connected-p'.\"\n00187|   (opencode-server--connected-p))\n00188| \n00189| (defun opencode-server--ensure ()\n00190|   \"Ensure the server is connected, auto-connecting if possible.\n00191| If `opencode-server-port' is set but we haven't connected yet,\n00192| automatically connect to the existing server.  Otherwise signal\n00193| a `user-error' with actionable instructions.\"\n00194|   (unless (opencode-server-connected-p)\n00195|     ;; Try auto-connect if a fixed port is configured\n00196|     (when (and opencode-server-port (not opencode-server--port))\n00197|       (opencode-server-start))\n00198|     ;; Check again after potential auto-connect\n00199|     (unless opencode-server--port\n00200|       (user-error \"OpenCode server not connected.  Either:\\n  (setq opencode-server-port 4096)  ; then retry\\n  M-x opencode-start               ; to spawn/connect\"))\n00201|     (unless (eq opencode-server--status 'connected)\n00202|       (user-error \"OpenCode server status is `%s', expected `connected'\"\n00203|                   opencode-server--status))))\n00204| \n00205| (defun opencode-server-ensure ()\n00206|   \"Ensure the server is connected, auto-connecting if possible.\"\n00207|   (opencode-server--ensure))\n00208| \n00209| (defun opencode-server--ensure-stdout-buffer ()\n00210|   \"Ensure the stdout accumulation buffer exists and return it.\"\n00211|   (or (and opencode-server--stdout-buffer\n00212|            (buffer-live-p opencode-server--stdout-buffer)\n00213|            opencode-server--stdout-buffer)\n00214|       (setq opencode-server--stdout-buffer\n00215|             (let ((buf (generate-new-buffer \" *opencode-server-stdout*\")))\n00216|               (with-current-buffer buf\n00217|                 (set-buffer-multibyte t))\n00218|               buf))))\n00219| \n00220| (defun opencode-server--kill-stdout-buffer ()\n00221|   \"Kill the stdout accumulation buffer if it exists.\"\n00222|   (when (and opencode-server--stdout-buffer\n00223|              (buffer-live-p opencode-server--stdout-buffer))\n00224|     (kill-buffer opencode-server--stdout-buffer))\n00225|   (setq opencode-server--stdout-buffer nil))\n00226| \n00227| ;;; --- Process filter ---\n00228| \n00229| (defun opencode-server--process-filter (_process output)\n00230|   \"Process filter for the opencode server subprocess.\n00231| Accumulates partial lines and logs complete ones.\n00232| During startup, also scans for the port announcement.\"\n00233|   (let ((accum-buf (opencode-server--ensure-stdout-buffer)))\n00234|     (with-current-buffer accum-buf\n00235|       (goto-char (point-max))\n00236|       (insert output)\n00237|       (goto-char (point-min))\n00238|       (while (search-forward \"\\n\" nil t)\n00239|         (let* ((nl-pos (point))\n00240|                (line-end (1- nl-pos))\n00241|                (line (buffer-substring-no-properties (point-min) line-end)))\n00242|           (delete-region (point-min) nl-pos)\n00243|           (goto-char (point-min))\n00244|           (unless (string-empty-p line)\n00245|             (opencode-server--log \"%s\" line)\n00246|             (when (eq opencode-server--status 'starting)\n00247|               (opencode-server--try-parse-port line))))))))\n00248| \n00249| (defun opencode-server--try-parse-port (line)\n00250|   \"Try to parse the server port from LINE.\n00251| Looks for patterns like \\\"listening on http://127.0.0.1:PORT\\\" or\n00252| \\\"http://HOST:PORT\\\" or just a bare port announcement.\n00253| Only parses during startup (when status is `starting').\"\n00254|   (when (and (eq opencode-server--status 'starting)\n00255|              (string-match \"\\\\(?:listening on\\\\|http://[^:]+:\\\\)\\\\([0-9]+\\\\)\" line))\n00256|     (let ((port (string-to-number (match-string 1 line))))\n00257|       (when (> port 0)\n00258|         (setq opencode-server--port port)\n00259|         (opencode-server--log \"Parsed server port: %d\" port)\n00260|         (when opencode-server--port-callback\n00261|           (funcall opencode-server--port-callback port)\n00262|           (setq opencode-server--port-callback nil))))))\n00263| \n00264| ;;; --- Process sentinel ---\n00265| \n00266| (defun opencode-server--process-sentinel (_process event)\n00267|   \"Sentinel for the opencode server process.\n00268| _PROCESS is the server process, EVENT describes the status change.\"\n00269|   (let ((event-str (string-trim event)))\n00270|     (opencode-server--log \"Server process event: %s\" event-str)\n00271|     (cond\n00272|      ;; Process exited normally\n00273|      ((string-match-p \"finished\\\\|exited\" event-str)\n00274|       (setq opencode-server--status 'disconnected)\n00275|       (setq opencode-server--process nil)\n00276|       (opencode-server--log \"Server stopped\")\n00277|       (run-hooks 'opencode-server-disconnected-hook))\n00278|      ;; Process was killed or crashed\n00279|      ((string-match-p \"\\\\(?:killed\\\\|signal\\\\|abnormal\\\\|connection broken\\\\)\" event-str)\n00280|       (setq opencode-server--status 'error)\n00281|       (setq opencode-server--process nil)\n00282|       (opencode-server--log \"Server crashed: %s\" event-str)\n00283|       (run-hooks 'opencode-server-disconnected-hook)\n00284|       ;; Auto-restart if configured and we were in managed mode\n00285|       (when (and opencode-server-auto-restart opencode-server--managed-p)\n00286|         (opencode-server--schedule-restart))))))\n00287| \n00288| ;;; --- Auto-restart ---\n00289| \n00290| (defun opencode-server--schedule-restart ()\n00291|   \"Schedule a server restart after `opencode-server-restart-delay' seconds.\"\n00292|   (when opencode-server--restart-timer\n00293|     (cancel-timer opencode-server--restart-timer))\n00294|   (opencode-server--log \"Scheduling restart in %ds...\" opencode-server-restart-delay)\n00295|   (setq opencode-server--restart-timer\n00296|         (run-with-timer opencode-server-restart-delay nil\n00297|                         #'opencode-server--do-restart)))\n00298| \n00299| (defun opencode-server--do-restart ()\n00300|   \"Perform the actual server restart.\"\n00301|   (setq opencode-server--restart-timer nil)\n00302|   (opencode-server--log \"Attempting restart...\")\n00303|   (condition-case err\n00304|       (opencode-server-start)\n00305|     (error\n00306|      (opencode-server--log \"Restart failed: %s\" (error-message-string err)))))\n00307| \n00308| ;;; --- Health check ---\n00309| \n00310| (defun opencode-server-health-check (&optional callback)\n00311|   \"Check server health via GET /global/health.\n00312| If CALLBACK is non-nil, call it with the parsed response plist on success,\n00313| or nil on failure.  If CALLBACK is nil, return the response synchronously\n00314| or signal an error.\"\n00315|   (let ((url (format \"http://%s:%d/global/health\"\n00316|                      opencode-server-host opencode-server--port)))\n00317|     (if callback\n00318|         (url-retrieve\n00319|          url\n00320|          (lambda (status cb)\n00321|            (if (plist-get status :error)\n00322|                (funcall cb nil)\n00323|              (goto-char url-http-end-of-headers)\n00324|              (condition-case nil\n00325|                 (funcall cb (opencode--json-parse\n00326|                              (buffer-substring-no-properties (point) (point-max))))\n00327|                (error (funcall cb nil)))))\n00328|          (list callback)\n00329|          t)\n00330|       ;; Synchronous\n00331|       (let ((buf (url-retrieve-synchronously url t nil 5)))\n00332|         (if buf\n00333|             (unwind-protect\n00334|                 (with-current-buffer buf\n00335|                   (goto-char url-http-end-of-headers)\n00336|                   (opencode--json-parse\n00337|                    (buffer-substring-no-properties (point) (point-max))))\n00338|               (kill-buffer buf))\n00339|           (error \"Health check failed: no response\"))))))\n00340| \n00341| (defun opencode-server--wait-for-health ()\n00342|   \"Wait for the server to become healthy.\n00343| Retries up to `opencode-server-health-retries' times with exponential backoff.\"\n00344|   (let ((retries opencode-server-health-retries)\n00345|         (delay 0.2)\n00346|         (healthy nil))\n00347|     (while (and (> retries 0) (not healthy))\n00348|       (condition-case err\n00349|           (let ((resp (opencode-server-health-check)))\n00350|             (when (plist-get resp :healthy)\n00351|               (setq healthy t)))\n00352|         (error (opencode--debug \"opencode-server: health check retry error: %S\" err)))\n00353|       (unless healthy\n00354|         (setq retries (1- retries))\n00355|         (when (> retries 0)\n00356|           (sleep-for delay)\n00357|           (setq delay (min (* delay 2) 5.0)))))\n00358|     (unless healthy\n00359|       (error \"Server health check failed after %d retries\"\n00360|              opencode-server-health-retries))\n00361|     (opencode-server--log \"Server is healthy\")\n00362|     t))\n00363| \n00364| ;;; --- Start / Stop ---\n00365| \n00366| \n00367| (defun opencode-server-start (&optional directory)\n00368|   \"Start or connect to the OpenCode server.\n00369| If `opencode-server-port' is set, connect to an existing server (connect mode).\n00370| Otherwise, spawn a new `opencode serve' subprocess (managed mode).\n00371| Optional DIRECTORY specifies the project directory.\"\n00372|   (interactive)\n00373|   (when (opencode-server-connected-p)\n00374|     (opencode-server--log \"Server already connected on port %d\" opencode-server--port)\n00375|     (user-error \"Server already connected.  Use `opencode-server-stop' first\"))\n00376|   ;; Reset state\n00377|   (opencode-server--kill-stdout-buffer)\n00378|   (setq opencode-server--status 'starting)\n00379|   (if opencode-server-port\n00380|       ;; Connect mode: attach to existing server\n00381|       (opencode-server--connect-existing directory)\n00382|     ;; Managed mode: spawn subprocess\n00383|     (opencode-server--spawn directory)))\n00384| \n00385| (defun opencode-server--connect-existing (&optional _directory)\n00386|   \"Connect to an existing OpenCode server at the configured port.\"\n00387|   (setq opencode-server--managed-p nil\n00388|         opencode-server--port opencode-server-port)\n00389|   (opencode-server--log \"Connecting to existing server at %s:%d\"\n00390|                         opencode-server-host opencode-server--port)\n00391|   (condition-case err\n00392|       (progn\n00393|         (opencode-server--wait-for-health)\n00394|         (setq opencode-server--status 'connected)\n00395|         (opencode-server--log \"Connected to server\")\n00396|         (run-hooks 'opencode-server-connected-hook))\n00397|     (error\n00398|      (setq opencode-server--status 'error\n00399|            opencode-server--port nil)\n00400|      (signal (car err) (cdr err)))))\n00401| \n00402| (defun opencode-server--spawn (&optional directory)\n00403|   \"Spawn a new OpenCode server subprocess.\n00404| Optional DIRECTORY is the working directory for the server.\"\n00405|   (setq opencode-server--managed-p t)\n00406|   (let* ((default-directory (or directory default-directory))\n00407|          (args (append opencode-server-args\n00408|                        (list \"--log-level\" opencode-server-log-level\n00409|                              \"--hostname\" opencode-server-host)))\n00410|          (process-environment (copy-sequence process-environment))\n00411|          (buf (get-buffer-create opencode-server--log-buffer-name))\n00412|          (proc (apply #'start-process\n00413|                       \"opencode-server\" buf\n00414|                       opencode-server-command args)))\n00415|     (opencode-server--log \"Spawning: %s %s (in %s)\"\n00416|                           opencode-server-command\n00417|                           (string-join args \" \")\n00418|                           default-directory)\n00419|     (set-process-filter proc #'opencode-server--process-filter)\n00420|     (set-process-sentinel proc #'opencode-server--process-sentinel)\n00421|     (set-process-query-on-exit-flag proc nil)\n00422|     (setq opencode-server--process proc)\n00423|     ;; Wait for port to be parsed (with timeout)\n00424|     (let ((deadline (+ (float-time) 15.0)))\n00425|       (while (and (not opencode-server--port)\n00426|                   (< (float-time) deadline)\n00427|                   (process-live-p proc))\n00428|         (accept-process-output proc 0.1))\n00429|       (unless opencode-server--port\n00430|         (when (process-live-p proc)\n00431|           (delete-process proc))\n00432|         (setq opencode-server--status 'error\n00433|               opencode-server--process nil)\n00434|         (error \"Timed out waiting for server port\")))\n00435|     ;; Health check\n00436|     (condition-case err\n00437|         (progn\n00438|           (opencode-server--wait-for-health)\n00439|           (setq opencode-server--status 'connected)\n00440|           (opencode-server--log \"Server ready on port %d\" opencode-server--port)\n00441|           (run-hooks 'opencode-server-connected-hook))\n00442|       (error\n00443|        (when (process-live-p proc)\n00444|          (delete-process proc))\n00445|        (setq opencode-server--status 'error\n00446|              opencode-server--process nil\n00447|              opencode-server--port nil)\n00448|        (signal (car err) (cdr err))))))\n00449| \n00450| (defun opencode-server--stop ()\n00451|   \"Stop the OpenCode server (internal implementation).\n00452| In managed mode, sends POST /global/dispose and kills the process.\n00453| In connect mode, just disconnects (does not kill the external server).\"\n00454|   (when opencode-server--restart-timer\n00455|     (cancel-timer opencode-server--restart-timer)\n00456|     (setq opencode-server--restart-timer nil))\n00457|   (when (and opencode-server--port\n00458|             (eq opencode-server--status 'connected)\n00459|             ;; Only send dispose when we have a real managed process.\n00460|             ;; Without this guard, --stop called with port set but no process\n00461|             ;; would send POST /global/dispose to whatever is on that port,\n00462|             ;; potentially killing an unrelated server.\n00463|             opencode-server--managed-p\n00464|             opencode-server--process\n00465|             (process-live-p opencode-server--process))\n00466|     ;; Try graceful dispose\n00467|     (condition-case err\n00468|         (let* ((url (format \"http://%s:%d/global/dispose\"\n00469|                             opencode-server-host opencode-server--port))\n00470|                (url-request-method \"POST\")\n00471|                (url-request-extra-headers '((\"Content-Type\" . \"application/json\")))\n00472|                (buf (url-retrieve-synchronously url t nil 5)))\n00473|           (when buf (kill-buffer buf))\n00474|           (opencode-server--log \"Sent dispose request\"))\n00475|       (error (opencode--debug \"opencode-server: dispose request error: %S\" err))))\n00476|   ;; Kill process if managed\n00477|   (when (and opencode-server--process (process-live-p opencode-server--process))\n00478|     (let ((proc opencode-server--process))\n00479|       (set-process-sentinel proc #'ignore)\n00480|       (delete-process proc)\n00481|       (opencode-server--log \"Server process killed\")))\n00482|   ;; Reset state\n00483|   (setq opencode-server--process nil\n00484|         opencode-server--port nil\n00485|         opencode-server--status 'disconnected\n00486|         opencode-server--managed-p nil)\n00487|   (opencode-server--kill-stdout-buffer)\n00488|   (run-hooks 'opencode-server-disconnected-hook)\n00489|   (opencode-server--log \"Disconnected\"))\n00490| \n00491| (defun opencode-server-stop ()\n00492|   \"Stop the OpenCode server.\"\n00493|   (interactive)\n00494|   (opencode-server--stop))\n00495| \n00496| ;;; --- Hooks ---\n00497| \n00498| (defvar opencode-server-connected-hook nil\n00499|   \"Hook run when the server connection is established.\")\n00500| \n00501| (defvar opencode-server-disconnected-hook nil\n00502|   \"Hook run when the server connection is lost.\")\n00503| \n00504| (provide 'opencode-server)\n00505| ;;; opencode-server.el ends here\n00506| \n\n(End of file - total 506 lines)\n</file>\n\n[Project README: /Users/bytedance/Documents/opencode.el/README.md]\n","title":"opencode-server.el","metadata":{"preview":";;; opencode-server.el --- Server subprocess lifecycle for opencode.el -*- lexical-binding: t; -*-\n\n;; Copyright (C) 2025 opencode.el contributors\n;; SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\n\n;;; Commentary:\n\n;; Manages the OpenCode server subprocess lifecycle:\n;; - Spawn `opencode serve --port 0` and parse the dynamically assigned port\n;; - Process sentinel for crash detection and auto-restart\n;; - Health check with retry and exponential backoff\n;; - Graceful shutdown via POST /global/dispose\n;; - Connect mode for attaching to an existing server\n;; - Per-directory server tracking\n;;\n;; Emacs 30: Benefits from `fast-read-process-output' (native default\n;; process filter) for efficient subprocess stdout/stderr handling.\n;;\n;; WHY HTTP IS REQUIRED:\n;; The TUI avoids HTTP by running Server.App() (Hono) in a Bun Worker thread","truncated":false},"time":{"start":1772839853063,"end":1772839853083}}}}}}

# [07:31:09.123] chat: refresh skipped (busy) sid=ses_33d619ed8ffeM7IJNc5B58iJO7
# [07:31:10.214] chat: refreshing stale buffer *opencode: opencode.el/opencode-sever-start 卡頓排查*
