:sse {"directory":"/Users/bytedance/Documents/opencode.el","payload":{"type":"message.part.updated","properties":{"part":{"id":"prt_cc57dac2d001XM827vljXIGTu9","sessionID":"ses_33d619ed8ffeM7IJNc5B58iJO7","messageID":"msg_cc57d5c51001UdL5TUdq5P7Cko","type":"tool","callID":"toolu_vrtx_017DQ7kENt33wHFrayMHs9U7","tool":"read","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/bytedance/Documents/opencode.el/opencode.el"},"output":"<file>\n00001| ;;; opencode.el --- Emacs 30 frontend for OpenCode AI agent -*- lexical-binding: t; -*-\n00002| \n00003| ;; Copyright (C) 2025 opencode.el contributors\n00004| ;; Author: opencode.el contributors\n00005| ;; Version: 0.1.0\n00006| ;; Package-Requires: ((emacs \"30.1\") (markdown-mode \"2.6\"))\n00007| ;; Keywords: tools, ai, coding\n00008| ;; URL: https://github.com/user/opencode.el\n00009| ;; SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\n00010| \n00011| ;;; Commentary:\n00012| \n00013| ;; Emacs 30 frontend for the OpenCode AI coding agent.\n00014| ;; Talks to the OpenCode HTTP REST API and SSE event stream.\n00015| ;;\n00016| ;; Usage:\n00017| ;;   M-x opencode-start    — Start the OpenCode server and connect\n00018| ;;   M-x opencode-chat     — Open a chat buffer for a session\n00019| ;;   C-c o                 — Global command prefix (customizable)\n00020| ;;\n00021| ;; Features:\n00022| ;;   - Spawns and manages the `opencode serve' subprocess\n00023| ;;   - Real-time streaming via Server-Sent Events (SSE)\n00024| ;;   - Session management with project grouping\n00025| ;;   - Chat buffer with message rendering (text, tool calls, reasoning)\n00026| ;;   - Floating frames and side window display modes\n00027| ;;\n00028| ;; Emacs 30 features used:\n00029| ;;   - Native JSON (json-parse-string / json-serialize)\n00030| ;;   - Native tree-sitter (for future code block highlighting)\n00031| ;;   - visual-wrap-prefix-mode, visual-line-mode\n00032| ;;   - mode-line-format-right-align\n00033| ;;   - fast-read-process-output (default for SSE streaming)\n00034| ;;   - display-buffer category entry\n00035| ;;   - Styled underlines (dashes, dots, double-line)\n00036| ;;   - font-lock-*-face (number, bracket, escape, function-call)\n00037| ;;   - seq-keep, seq-do-indexed\n00038| \n00039| ;;; Code:\n00040| \n00041| (require 'opencode-log)\n00042| (require 'opencode-markdown)\n00043| (require 'opencode-faces)\n00044| (require 'opencode-server)\n00045| (require 'opencode-api)\n00046| (require 'opencode-agent)\n00047| (require 'opencode-config)\n00048| (require 'opencode-sse)\n00049| (require 'opencode-ui)\n00050| (require 'opencode-window)\n00051| (require 'opencode-session)\n00052| (require 'opencode-chat)\n00053| (require 'opencode-command)\n00054| (require 'opencode-transient)\n00055| (require 'opencode-permission)\n00056| (require 'opencode-question)\n00057| (require 'opencode-todo)\n00058| (require 'opencode-sidebar)\n00059| (require 'project)\n00060| (require 'seq)\n00061| \n00062| ;;; --- Customization group ---\n00063| \n00064| (defgroup opencode nil\n00065|   \"Emacs frontend for the OpenCode AI coding agent.\"\n00066|   :group 'tools\n00067|   :prefix \"opencode-\"\n00068|   :link '(url-link :tag \"Homepage\" \"https://github.com/user/opencode.el\"))\n00069| \n00070| ;;; --- Customization ---\n00071| \n00072| (defcustom opencode-keymap-prefix \"C-c o\"\n00073|   \"Prefix key for the opencode command map.\n00074| Change this before loading opencode.el or call\n00075| `opencode--setup-keymap' after changing.\"\n00076|   :type 'string\n00077|   :group 'opencode)\n00078| \n00079| (defcustom opencode-default-directory nil\n00080|   \"Default project directory for OpenCode.\n00081| When nil, uses `default-directory' of the current buffer.\"\n00082|   :type '(choice (const :tag \"Use current buffer directory\" nil)\n00083|                  (directory :tag \"Fixed directory\"))\n00084|   :group 'opencode)\n00085| \n00086| ;;; --- Version ---\n00087| \n00088| (defconst opencode-version \"0.1.0\"\n00089|   \"Version of opencode.el.\")\n00090| \n00091| ;;; --- Global command map ---\n00092| \n00093| (defvar-keymap opencode-command-map\n00094|   :doc \"Global opencode commands (bound under opencode-keymap-prefix).\"\n00095|   \"o\" #'opencode-start\n00096|   \"c\" #'opencode-chat\n00097|   \"l\" #'opencode-list-sessions\n00098|   \"n\" #'opencode-new-session\n00099|   \"a\" #'opencode-abort\n00100|   \"t\" #'opencode-toggle-sidebar\n00101|   \"q\" #'opencode-disconnect\n00102|   \"r\" #'opencode-refresh\n00103|   \"?\" #'opencode-transient)\n00104| \n00105| ;;; --- Minor mode ---\n00106| \n00107| (defvar opencode-mode-map\n00108|   (let ((map (make-sparse-keymap)))\n00109|     (keymap-set map opencode-keymap-prefix opencode-command-map)\n00110|     map)\n00111|   \"Keymap for `opencode-mode'.\")\n00112| \n00113| (defvar opencode--modeline-string nil\n00114|   \"Current modeline string for opencode-mode.\")\n00115| \n00116| (defun opencode--modeline-update ()\n00117|   \"Update the modeline string based on connection state.\"\n00118|   (setq opencode--modeline-string\n00119|         (cond\n00120|          ((opencode-server-connected-p)\n00121|           (propertize \" OC:connected\" 'face 'success))\n00122|          ((eq opencode-server--status 'starting)\n00123|           (propertize \" OC:starting\" 'face 'warning))\n00124|          (t\n00125|           (propertize \" OC:off\" 'face 'shadow))))\n00126|   (force-mode-line-update t))\n00127| \n00128| ;;;###autoload\n00129| (define-minor-mode opencode-mode\n00130|   \"Global minor mode for the OpenCode AI coding agent.\n00131| When enabled, provides the `C-c o' prefix keymap and modeline indicator.\n00132| \n00133| \\\\{opencode-mode-map}\"\n00134|   :global t\n00135|   :lighter opencode--modeline-string\n00136|   :keymap opencode-mode-map\n00137|   :group 'opencode\n00138|   (if opencode-mode\n00139|       (progn\n00140|         (opencode--modeline-update)\n00141|         (add-hook 'opencode-server-connected-hook #'opencode--on-connected)\n00142|         (add-hook 'opencode-server-disconnected-hook #'opencode--on-disconnected))\n00143|     (remove-hook 'opencode-server-connected-hook #'opencode--on-connected)\n00144|     (remove-hook 'opencode-server-disconnected-hook #'opencode--on-disconnected)\n00145|     (opencode--modeline-update)))\n00146| \n00147| ;;; --- Hooks ---\n00148| \n00149| (defun opencode--on-connected ()\n00150|   \"Handle server connected event.\"\n00151|   (opencode--modeline-update)\n00152|   (message \"OpenCode: connected on port %s\" opencode-server--port)\n00153|   ;; Fetch agent/model info so header-line shows correct values\n00154|   (opencode-api--fetch-agent-info)\n00155|   ;; Pre-warm commands cache so slash completion is instant\n00156|   (opencode-config-prewarm)\n00157|   ;; Always connect SSE when server connects\n00158|   (opencode-sse-connect))\n00159| \n00160| (defun opencode--on-disconnected ()\n00161|   \"Handle server disconnected event.\"\n00162|   (opencode--modeline-update)\n00163|   (message \"OpenCode: disconnected\"))\n00164| \n00165| ;;; --- Interactive commands ---\n00166| \n00167| (defun opencode--ensure-directory ()\n00168|   \"Ensure `opencode-default-directory' is set to the project root.\n00169| When nil, resolves using the same order as `opencode-start':\n00170|   1. Project root via `project-current' (detects .git, .hg, etc.)\n00171|   2. `default-directory' of the current buffer\n00172| Called from entry points that may bypass `opencode-start' (e.g.\n00173| `opencode-chat', `opencode-new-session').\"\n00174|   (unless opencode-default-directory\n00175|     (setopt opencode-default-directory\n00176|             (directory-file-name\n00177|              (expand-file-name\n00178|               (or (when-let* ((proj (project-current t)))\n00179|                     (project-root proj))\n00180|                   default-directory))))))\n00181| \n00182| (defun opencode--ensure-ready ()\n00183|   \"Ensure opencode-mode is active and server is connected.\n00184| Activates `opencode-mode' if not already active, ensures the server\n00185| is connected, and sets the project directory.\"\n00186|   (unless opencode-mode (opencode-mode 1))\n00187|   (opencode-server-ensure)\n00188|   (opencode--ensure-directory))\n00189| \n00190| \n00191| ;;;###autoload\n00192| (defun opencode-start (&optional directory)\n00193|   \"Start the OpenCode server and connect.\n00194| DIRECTORY is the project directory.  When nil, the resolution order is:\n00195|   1. `opencode-default-directory' (if set)\n00196|   2. Project root via `project-current' (detects .git, .hg, etc.)\n00197|   3. `default-directory' of the current buffer\n00198| When called interactively with a prefix argument, prompts for the directory.\"\n00199|   (interactive\n00200|    (list (when current-prefix-arg\n00201|            (read-directory-name \"OpenCode project directory: \"))))\n00202|   (unless opencode-mode\n00203|     (opencode-mode 1))\n00204|   (let ((dir (directory-file-name\n00205|               (expand-file-name\n00206|                (or directory\n00207|                    opencode-default-directory\n00208|                    (when-let* ((proj (project-current t)))\n00209|                      (project-root proj))\n00210|                    default-directory)))))\n00211|     ;; Persist so API headers and sidebar use the same root\n00212|     (setopt opencode-default-directory dir)\n00213|     (opencode-server-start dir)\n00214|     ;; Open session list after server is ready (one-shot)\n00215|     (add-hook 'opencode-server-connected-hook #'opencode--open-session-list)))\n00216| \n00217| (defun opencode--open-session-list ()\n00218|   \"Open the session list after server is ready.\n00219| Runs once, then removes itself from the hook.\"\n00220|   (remove-hook 'opencode-server-connected-hook #'opencode--open-session-list)\n00221|   (opencode-session-open-list opencode-default-directory))\n00222| \n00223| ;;;###autoload\n00224| (defun opencode-chat (&optional session-id)\n00225|   \"Open a chat buffer for SESSION-ID.\n00226| If SESSION-ID is nil, prompts for a session from the list.\n00227| The completion list includes a \\\"\\u2605 New session\\\" option at the top.\n00228| Auto-connects to the server if `opencode-server-port' is set.\n00229| The session list is scoped to the current buffer's project (via\n00230| `project-current'), so switching projects shows the right sessions.\"\n00231|   (interactive)\n00232|   (opencode--ensure-ready)\n00233|   ;; Use current buffer's project so the session list is scoped correctly\n00234|   ;; even when opencode-default-directory points to a different project.\n00235|   (let* ((current-dir (or (when-let ((proj (project-current)))\n00236|                             (project-root proj))\n00237|                           opencode-default-directory\n00238|                           default-directory))\n00239|          (opencode-default-directory current-dir)\n00240|          (result (or (and session-id (cons session-id nil))\n00241|                      (opencode--read-session \"Chat session: \"))))\n00242|     (when result\n00243|       (if (eq result 'new)\n00244|           (opencode-new-session)\n00245|         (opencode-chat-open (car result) (or (cdr result)\n00246|                                                current-dir))))))\n00247| \n00248| ;;;###autoload\n00249| (defun opencode-list-sessions ()\n00250|   \"Open the session list buffer for the current project.\"\n00251|   (interactive)\n00252|   (opencode--ensure-ready)\n00253|   (opencode-session-open-list\n00254|    (or (when-let* ((proj (project-current)))\n00255|          (project-root proj))\n00256|        opencode-default-directory\n00257|        default-directory)))\n00258| \n00259| ;;;###autoload\n00260| (defun opencode-new-session (&optional title)\n00261|   \"Create a new session with optional TITLE and open it.\"\n00262|   (interactive \"sSession title (empty for untitled): \")\n00263|   (opencode--ensure-ready)\n00264|   (let* ((title (if (and title (not (string-empty-p title))) title nil))\n00265|          (session (opencode-session-create title)))\n00266|     (when session\n00267|       (opencode-chat-open (plist-get session :id)\n00268|                           (plist-get session :directory)))))\n00269| \n00270| ;;;###autoload\n00271| (defalias 'opencode-chat-new #'opencode-new-session\n00272|   \"Create a new chat session and open it.\n00273| Alias for `opencode-new-session'.\")\n00274| \n00275| ;;;###autoload\n00276| (defun opencode-abort ()\n00277|   \"Abort the current generation in the active chat buffer.\"\n00278|   (interactive)\n00279|   (let ((buf (opencode--current-chat-buffer)))\n00280|     (if buf\n00281|         (with-current-buffer buf\n00282|           (opencode-chat-abort))\n00283|       (message \"No active chat buffer\"))))\n00284| \n00285| ;;;###autoload\n00286| (defun opencode-toggle-sidebar ()\n00287|   \"Toggle the OpenCode session sidebar.\"\n00288|   (interactive)\n00289|   (opencode-window-toggle-sidebar))\n00290| \n00291| ;;;###autoload\n00292| (defun opencode-disconnect ()\n00293|   \"Disconnect from the OpenCode server and stop the subprocess.\"\n00294|   (interactive)\n00295|   (opencode-sse--disconnect)\n00296|   (opencode-server--stop)\n00297|   (opencode--modeline-update)\n00298|   (message \"OpenCode: disconnected\"))\n00299| \n00300| ;;;###autoload\n00301| (defun opencode-refresh ()\n00302|   \"Refresh all cached data from the server.\n00303| Invalidates agent, config, and command caches, re-fetches everything,\n00304| and schedules a refresh for all open chat and sidebar buffers.\n00305| Same behavior as `server.instance.disposed'.\"\n00306|   (interactive)\n00307|   (opencode--do-rebootstrap)\n00308|   (message \"OpenCode: refreshed\"))\n00309| ;;; --- Timer helpers ---\n00310| \n00311| (defun opencode--debounce (timer-sym delay fn &optional idle)\n00312|   \"Cancel timer in TIMER-SYM, schedule FN after DELAY seconds.\n00313| If IDLE is non-nil, use `run-with-idle-timer' instead of `run-with-timer'.\n00314| FN runs in the current buffer if still alive.\"\n00315|   (when (timerp (symbol-value timer-sym))\n00316|     (cancel-timer (symbol-value timer-sym)))\n00317|   (let ((buf (current-buffer)))\n00318|     (set timer-sym\n00319|          (funcall (if idle #'run-with-idle-timer #'run-with-timer)\n00320|                   delay nil\n00321|                   (lambda ()\n00322|                     (when (buffer-live-p buf)\n00323|                       (with-current-buffer buf\n00324|                         (set timer-sym nil)\n00325|                         (funcall fn))))))))\n00326| \n00327| ;;; --- Helpers ---\n00328| (defun opencode--read-session (prompt)\n00329|   \"Read a session with completing-read using PROMPT.\n00330| Returns a cons (ID . DIRECTORY) for a selected session, the symbol\n00331| `new' for a new session, or nil.\n00332| The \\\"★ New session\\\" option is always available at the top.\"\n00333|   (let* ((sessions (opencode-session--list))\n00334|          (session-candidates\n00335|           (when sessions\n00336|             (mapcar\n00337|              (lambda (s)\n00338|                (let ((id (plist-get s :id))\n00339|                      (title (or (plist-get s :title) \"(untitled)\"))\n00340|                      (dir (or (plist-get s :directory) \"\")))\n00341|                  (cons (format \"%s — %s (%s)\"\n00342|                                title\n00343|                                (file-name-nondirectory\n00344|                                 (directory-file-name dir))\n00345|                                id)\n00346|                        (cons id dir))))\n00347|              (append sessions nil))))\n00348|          (candidates (cons '(\"★ New session\" . new) session-candidates))\n00349|          (choice (completing-read prompt candidates nil t)))\n00350|     (alist-get choice candidates nil nil #'equal)))\n00351| \n00352| (defun opencode--current-chat-buffer ()\n00353|   \"Return the current or most recent chat buffer, or nil.\n00354| Prefers the current buffer if it is a chat buffer, then\n00355| checks the registry, falling back to `buffer-list' scan.\"\n00356|   (or (and (eq major-mode 'opencode-chat-mode) (current-buffer))\n00357|       (car (opencode--all-chat-buffers))\n00358|       (seq-find\n00359|        (lambda (buf)\n00360|          (with-current-buffer buf\n00361|            (eq major-mode 'opencode-chat-mode)))\n00362|        (buffer-list))))\n00363| \n00364| ;;; --- Buffer registry ---\n00365| \n00366| (defvar opencode--chat-registry (make-hash-table :test 'equal)\n00367|   \"Hash table mapping session-id (string) to chat buffer.\n00368| Used for O(1) dispatch of SSE events to the correct chat buffer.\")\n00369| \n00370| (defvar opencode--sidebar-registry (make-hash-table :test 'equal)\n00371|   \"Hash table mapping project-dir (string, normalized) to sidebar buffer.\n00372| Used for O(1) dispatch of SSE events to the correct sidebar buffer.\")\n00373| \n00374| (defun opencode--register-chat-buffer (session-id buffer)\n00375|   \"Register BUFFER as the chat buffer for SESSION-ID.\n00376| Skips registration if SESSION-ID is nil or BUFFER is not live.\"\n00377|   (when (and session-id (buffer-live-p buffer))\n00378|     (puthash session-id buffer opencode--chat-registry)))\n00379| \n00380| (defun opencode--deregister-chat-buffer (session-id)\n00381|   \"Deregister chat buffer for SESSION-ID.\"\n00382|   (remhash session-id opencode--chat-registry))\n00383| \n00384| (defun opencode--register-sidebar-buffer (project-dir buffer)\n00385|   \"Register BUFFER as the sidebar buffer for PROJECT-DIR.\n00386| Normalizes PROJECT-DIR to absolute path before storing.\"\n00387|   (when project-dir\n00388|     (puthash (expand-file-name project-dir) buffer opencode--sidebar-registry)))\n00389| \n00390| (defun opencode--deregister-sidebar-buffer (project-dir)\n00391|   \"Deregister sidebar buffer for PROJECT-DIR.\n00392| Normalizes PROJECT-DIR to absolute path before lookup.\"\n00393|   (when project-dir\n00394|     (remhash (expand-file-name project-dir) opencode--sidebar-registry)))\n00395| \n00396| (defun opencode--chat-buffer-for-session (session-id)\n00397|   \"Return chat buffer registered for SESSION-ID, or nil if not found.\n00398| Auto-deregisters if buffer has been killed.\"\n00399|   (let ((buf (gethash session-id opencode--chat-registry)))\n00400|     (if (and buf (buffer-live-p buf))\n00401|         buf\n00402|       (progn\n00403|         (opencode--deregister-chat-buffer session-id)\n00404|         nil))))\n00405| \n00406| (defun opencode--sidebar-buffer-for-project (project-dir)\n00407|   \"Return sidebar buffer registered for PROJECT-DIR, or nil if not found.\n00408| Normalizes PROJECT-DIR to absolute path for lookup.\n00409| Auto-deregisters if buffer has been killed.\"\n00410|   (when project-dir\n00411|     (let ((buf (gethash (expand-file-name project-dir) opencode--sidebar-registry)))\n00412|       (if (and buf (buffer-live-p buf))\n00413|           buf\n00414|         (opencode--deregister-sidebar-buffer project-dir)\n00415|         nil))))\n00416| \n00417| (defun opencode--all-chat-buffers ()\n00418|   \"Return list of all live chat buffers in registry.\n00419| Auto-deregisters any entries with killed buffers.\"\n00420|   (let ((live-buffers '()))\n00421|     (maphash (lambda (session-id buf)\n00422|                (if (buffer-live-p buf)\n00423|                    (push buf live-buffers)\n00424|                  (opencode--deregister-chat-buffer session-id)))\n00425|              opencode--chat-registry)\n00426|     live-buffers))\n00427| \n00428| (defun opencode--all-sidebar-buffers ()\n00429|   \"Return list of all live sidebar buffers in registry.\n00430| Auto-deregisters any entries with killed buffers.\"\n00431|   (let ((live-buffers '()))\n00432|     (maphash (lambda (project-dir buf)\n00433|                (if (buffer-live-p buf)\n00434|                    (push buf live-buffers)\n00435|                  (opencode--deregister-sidebar-buffer project-dir)))\n00436|              opencode--sidebar-registry)\n00437|     live-buffers))\n00438| \n00439| (defun opencode--dispatch-to-chat-buffer (session-id handler event)\n00440|   \"Call HANDLER with EVENT in the chat buffer registered for SESSION-ID.\n00441| Does nothing if buffer not found or not live. Wraps handler in `condition-case'\n00442|  to handle errors during SSE storms.\"\n00443|   (let ((buf (opencode--chat-buffer-for-session session-id)))\n00444|     (when buf\n00445|       (with-current-buffer buf\n00446|         (condition-case err\n00447|             (funcall handler event)\n00448|           (error (opencode--debug \"dispatch: handler error in %s: %S\" (buffer-name) err)))))))\n00449| \n00450| (defun opencode--dispatch-to-all-chat-buffers (handler event)\n00451|   \"Call HANDLER with EVENT in every live chat buffer.\n00452| Wraps each call in `condition-case' to handle errors.\"\n00453|   (dolist (buf (opencode--all-chat-buffers))\n00454|     (with-current-buffer buf\n00455|       (condition-case err\n00456|           (funcall handler event)\n00457|         (error (opencode--debug \"dispatch: handler error in %s: %S\" (buffer-name) err))))))\n00458| \n00459| (defun opencode--dispatch-to-sidebar-buffer (project-dir handler event)\n00460|   \"Call HANDLER with EVENT in the sidebar buffer registered for PROJECT-DIR.\n00461| Does nothing if buffer not found or not live. Wraps handler in `condition-case'\n00462|  to handle errors during SSE storms.\"\n00463|   (let ((buf (opencode--sidebar-buffer-for-project project-dir)))\n00464|     (when buf\n00465|       (with-current-buffer buf\n00466|         (condition-case err\n00467|             (funcall handler event)\n00468|           (error (opencode--debug \"dispatch: handler error in %s: %S\" (buffer-name) err)))))))\n00469| \n00470| (defun opencode--dispatch-to-all-sidebar-buffers (handler event)\n00471|   \"Call HANDLER with EVENT in every live sidebar buffer.\n00472| Wraps each call in `condition-case' to handle errors.\"\n00473|   (dolist (buf (opencode--all-sidebar-buffers))\n00474|     (with-current-buffer buf\n00475|       (condition-case err\n00476|           (funcall handler event)\n00477|         (error (opencode--debug \"dispatch: handler error in %s: %S\" (buffer-name) err))))))\n00478| \n00479| \n00480| ;;; --- Centralized SSE hook registration ---\n00481| \n00482| ;; All SSE event → handler wiring lives here.  Each module only exposes\n00483| ;; handler functions (opencode-chat--on-*, opencode-sidebar--on-*, etc.).\n00484| ;; opencode.el owns the dispatch: it knows about the registry, knows all\n00485| ;; modules, and wires hooks at load time.  No module registers its own\n00486| ;; SSE hooks — this eliminates declare-function hacks, fboundp guards,\n00487| ;; and duplicated dispatch-fallback logic.\n00488| ;;\n00489| ;; No declare-function needed: all modules are already `require'd above.\n00490| \n00491| (defun opencode--dispatch-chat-event (handler event)\n00492|   \"Dispatch a session-scoped SSE EVENT to the correct chat buffer.\n00493| HANDLER is the chat handler function to call.\n00494| Uses O(1) registry lookup by session-id, with buffer-list fallback.\"\n00495|   (let ((sid (opencode-chat--session-id-from-event event)))\n00496|     (cond\n00497|      ;; Session-scoped: O(1) registry lookup\n00498|      (sid\n00499|       (when-let* ((buf (opencode--chat-buffer-for-session sid)))\n00500|           (opencode--dispatch-to-chat-buffer sid handler event)))\n00501|      ;; No session-id: broadcast to all chat buffers\n00502|      (t\n00503|       (opencode--dispatch-to-all-chat-buffers handler event)))))\n00504| \n00505| (defun opencode--dispatch-popup-event (handler event)\n00506|   \"Dispatch a popup SSE EVENT to the correct chat buffer.\n00507| Like `opencode--dispatch-chat-event' but with child→parent fallback:\n00508| if no buffer is found for the event's session-id, checks if it is a\n00509| child session and falls back to the parent session's buffer.\"\n00510|   (let* ((sid (opencode-chat--session-id-from-event event))\n00511|          (buf (when sid (opencode--chat-buffer-for-session sid))))\n00512|     (cond\n00513|      (buf\n00514|       (opencode--dispatch-to-chat-buffer sid handler event))\n00515|      ;; Child→parent fallback: check in-memory cache, then sync HTTP\n00516|      (sid\n00517|       (when-let* ((parent-id\n00518|                    (or (opencode-chat-message-child-parent-get sid)\n00519|                        (opencode-chat--session-parent-id sid)))\n00520|                   (parent-buf (opencode--chat-buffer-for-session parent-id)))\n00521|         (with-current-buffer parent-buf\n00522|           (condition-case err\n00523|               (funcall handler event)\n00524|             (error (opencode--debug \"dispatch-popup: handler error in %s: %S\"\n00525|                                     (buffer-name) err))))))\n00526|      ;; No session-id: broadcast\n00527|      (t\n00528|       (opencode--dispatch-to-all-chat-buffers handler event)))))\n00529| \n00530| (defun opencode--dispatch-sidebar-event (event)\n00531|   \"Dispatch a session SSE EVENT to the correct sidebar buffer.\n00532| Uses the event :directory field for O(1) registry lookup.\n00533| Falls back to broadcasting if no directory is present.\"\n00534|   (let ((dir (plist-get event :directory)))\n00535|     (cond\n00536|      ;; O(1) registry path\n00537|      (dir\n00538|       (opencode--dispatch-to-sidebar-buffer\n00539|        dir #'opencode-sidebar--on-session-event event))\n00540|      ;; No directory: broadcast to all sidebars\n00541|      (t\n00542|       (opencode--dispatch-to-all-sidebar-buffers\n00543|        #'opencode-sidebar--on-session-event event)))))\n00544| \n00545| (defconst opencode--sse-chat-dispatch-specs\n00546|   '(;; SSE hook                                      Chat handler\n00547|     (opencode-sse-message-updated-hook             . opencode-chat--on-message-updated)\n00548|     (opencode-sse-message-removed-hook             . opencode-chat--on-message-removed)\n00549|     (opencode-sse-message-part-updated-hook        . opencode-chat--on-part-updated)\n00550|     (opencode-sse-session-updated-hook             . opencode-chat--on-session-updated)\n00551|     (opencode-sse-session-status-hook              . opencode-chat--on-session-status)\n00552|     (opencode-sse-session-idle-hook                . opencode-chat--on-session-idle)\n00553|     (opencode-sse-session-diff-hook                . opencode-chat--on-session-diff)\n00554|     (opencode-sse-session-deleted-hook             . opencode-chat--on-session-deleted)\n00555|     (opencode-sse-session-error-hook               . opencode-chat--on-session-error)\n00556|     (opencode-sse-session-compacted-hook           . opencode-chat--on-session-compacted)\n00557|     (opencode-sse-server-instance-disposed-hook    . opencode-chat--on-server-instance-disposed)\n00558|     (opencode-sse-installation-update-available-hook . opencode-chat--on-installation-update-available))\n00559|   \"Alist mapping SSE hooks to chat handler functions for dispatch registration.\")\n00560| \n00561| ;; Register all SSE hooks at load time.\n00562| ;; Chat: dispatch via session-id registry\n00563| (dolist (spec opencode--sse-chat-dispatch-specs)\n00564|   (let ((hook (car spec))\n00565|         (handler (cdr spec)))\n00566|     (add-hook hook\n00567|               (lambda (event)\n00568|                 (opencode--dispatch-chat-event handler event)))))\n00569| \n00570| ;; Sidebar: session events dispatch via directory registry\n00571| (dolist (hook '(opencode-sse-session-updated-hook\n00572|                 opencode-sse-session-status-hook\n00573|                 opencode-sse-session-idle-hook\n00574|                 opencode-sse-session-deleted-hook))\n00575|   (add-hook hook #'opencode--dispatch-sidebar-event))\n00576| \n00577| ;; Permission: asked → dispatch to chat buffer (pending queue is buffer-local)\n00578| ;; Uses popup dispatch with child→parent fallback.\n00579| ;; replied → global (must find popup in any buffer to dismiss it)\n00580| (add-hook 'opencode-sse-permission-asked-hook\n00581|           (lambda (event)\n00582|             (opencode--dispatch-popup-event #'opencode-permission--on-asked event)))\n00583| (add-hook 'opencode-sse-permission-replied-hook #'opencode-permission--on-replied)\n00584| \n00585| ;; Question: asked → dispatch to chat buffer (pending queue is buffer-local)\n00586| ;; Uses popup dispatch with child→parent fallback.\n00587| ;; replied/rejected → global (must find popup in any buffer to dismiss it)\n00588| (add-hook 'opencode-sse-question-asked-hook\n00589|           (lambda (event)\n00590|             (opencode--dispatch-popup-event #'opencode-question--on-asked event)))\n00591| (add-hook 'opencode-sse-question-replied-hook #'opencode-question--on-replied)\n00592| (add-hook 'opencode-sse-question-rejected-hook #'opencode-question--on-rejected)\n00593| \n00594| ;; Todo: global hook\n00595| (add-hook 'opencode-sse-todo-updated-hook #'opencode-todo--on-updated)\n00596| \n00597| ;; SSE internal: rebootstrap on disposal (debounced)\n00598| (defvar opencode--rebootstrap-timer nil\n00599|   \"Timer for debounced re-bootstrap after server disposal.\")\n00600| \n00601| (defun opencode--do-rebootstrap ()\n00602|   \"Re-bootstrap after server instance disposal.\n00603| Re-fetches agent info and refreshes all chat/sidebar buffers.\n00604| Chat buffers are refreshed directly via `opencode-chat--refresh' (not\n00605| the debounced schedule-refresh) because `refresh' has a built-in busy\n00606| guard that skips the fetch when the session is streaming, marking the\n00607| buffer stale instead.  This prevents slow /message requests against a\n00608| busy server.  This does NOT disconnect SSE — the connection stays alive.\"\n00609|   (opencode--debug \"opencode: re-bootstrap starting\")\n00610|   ;; 1. Invalidate agent cache — lazy re-fetch on next access\n00611|   (opencode-agent-invalidate)\n00612|   ;; 2. Refresh all chat buffers via registry\n00613|   (dolist (buf (opencode--all-chat-buffers))\n00614|     (when (buffer-live-p buf)\n00615|       (with-current-buffer buf\n00616|         (opencode-chat--refresh))))\n00617|   ;; 3. Schedule refresh for all sidebar buffers via registry\n00618|   (dolist (buf (opencode--all-sidebar-buffers))\n00619|     (when (buffer-live-p buf)\n00620|       (with-current-buffer buf\n00621|         (opencode-sidebar--schedule-refresh))))\n00622|   (opencode--debug \"opencode: re-bootstrap complete\"))\n00623| \n00624| (defun opencode--on-instance-disposed (event)\n00625|   \"Handle `server.instance.disposed' EVENT by triggering re-bootstrap.\n00626| Debounced to 0.5s to avoid flicker on rapid disposal events.\"\n00627|   (let ((dir (plist-get (plist-get event :properties) :directory)))\n00628|     (opencode--debug \"opencode: instance disposed for %s, scheduling rebootstrap\" dir))\n00629|   (when opencode--rebootstrap-timer\n00630|     (cancel-timer opencode--rebootstrap-timer))\n00631|   (setq opencode--rebootstrap-timer\n00632|         (run-with-idle-timer 0.5 nil #'opencode--do-rebootstrap)))\n00633| \n00634| (defun opencode--on-global-disposed (_event)\n00635|   \"Handle `global.disposed' EVENT by re-bootstrapping.\n00636| Uses the same debounced timer as `on-instance-disposed' so that rapid\n00637| sequences coalesce into a single re-bootstrap.\"\n00638|   (opencode--debug \"opencode: global.disposed — scheduling rebootstrap\")\n00639|   (when opencode--rebootstrap-timer\n00640|     (cancel-timer opencode--rebootstrap-timer))\n00641|   (setq opencode--rebootstrap-timer\n00642|         (run-with-idle-timer 0.5 nil #'opencode--do-rebootstrap)))\n00643| \n00644| (add-hook 'opencode-sse-server-instance-disposed-hook #'opencode--on-instance-disposed)\n00645| (add-hook 'opencode-sse-global-disposed-hook #'opencode--on-global-disposed)\n00646| \n00647| ;;; --- Cleanup ---\n00648| \n00649| (defun opencode-cleanup ()\n00650|   \"Kill all OpenCode buffers and stop the server.\n00651| Use this to fully reset the OpenCode state.\"\n00652|   (interactive)\n00653|   (clrhash opencode--chat-registry)\n00654|   (clrhash opencode--sidebar-registry)\n00655|   (opencode-sse--disconnect)\n00656|   (opencode-server--stop)\n00657|   (dolist (buf (buffer-list))\n00658|     (when (string-prefix-p \"*opencode:\" (buffer-name buf))\n00659|       (kill-buffer buf)))\n00660|   (opencode--modeline-update)\n00661|   (message \"OpenCode: cleaned up\"))\n00662| \n00663| (provide 'opencode)\n00664| ;;; opencode.el ends here\n00665| \n\n(End of file - total 665 lines)\n</file>","title":"opencode.el","metadata":{"preview":";;; opencode.el --- Emacs 30 frontend for OpenCode AI agent -*- lexical-binding: t; -*-\n\n;; Copyright (C) 2025 opencode.el contributors\n;; Author: opencode.el contributors\n;; Version: 0.1.0\n;; Package-Requires: ((emacs \"30.1\") (markdown-mode \"2.6\"))\n;; Keywords: tools, ai, coding\n;; URL: https://github.com/user/opencode.el\n;; SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\n\n;;; Commentary:\n\n;; Emacs 30 frontend for the OpenCode AI coding agent.\n;; Talks to the OpenCode HTTP REST API and SSE event stream.\n;;\n;; Usage:\n;;   M-x opencode-start    — Start the OpenCode server and connect\n;;   M-x opencode-chat     — Open a chat buffer for a session\n;;   C-c o                 — Global command prefix (customizable)\n;;","truncated":false},"time":{"start":1772839874891,"end":1772839875700}}}}}}

# [07:31:15.754] session.status

`opencode-api--model-id' and `opencode-api--provider-id'.\n00338| Also ensures the agent cache is populated for downstream consumers.\"\n00339|   (opencode--debug \"opencode-api: fetching model info...\")\n00340|   (condition-case err\n00341|       (progn\n00342|         (opencode-agent--ensure-cache)\n00343|         (let* ((config (opencode-api-get-sync \"/config\"))\n00344| err)))))\n00352| \n00353| (defun opencode-api--valid-agent-p (agent-name)\n00354|   \"Return non-nil if AGENT-NAME is a valid agent known to the server.\n00355| Checks against the agent cache.  If the cache is empty, ensures it first.\"\n00356|   (opencode-agent--find-by-name agent-name))\n00357| \n00358| (defun opencode-api--prompt-body (text &optional agent model-id provider-id\n00359|                                        variant mentions images message-id context)\n00360|   \"Build the POST body for /session/:id/prompt_async.\n00361| TEXT is the user's message text.\n00362| AGENT, MODEL-ID, PROVIDER-ID, VARIANT are optional overrides.\n00363| When nil, uses global defaults from opencode-api--*.\n00364| MENTIONS is an optional list of mention plists, each with :type, :name,\n00365| :path (for files), :start, and :end (positions in TEXT).\n00366| When MENTIONS is non-nil, additional file/agent parts are included.\n00367| When VARIANT is non-nil, includes :variant in output plist.\n00368| IMAGES is an optional list of image plists, each with :data-url, :mime,\n00369| and :filename.  When non-nil, file parts with data: URLs are appended\n00370| after text and mention parts.\n00371| MESSAGE-ID is the optional parent message ID.\n00372| CONTEXT is an optional plist with :filename and :selection.\n00373| Returns a plist with :parts, :agent, :model, :variant, :messageID, :context.\"\n00374|   (let* ((text-part (list :type \"text\" :text text :id (opencode-util--generate-id \"prt\")))\n00375|          (mention-parts\n00376|           (when mentions\n00377|             (mapcar\n00378|              (lambda (m)\n00379|                (let ((mtype (plist-get m :type))\n00380|                      (mname (plist-get m :name))\n00381|                      (mpath (plist-get m :path))\n00382|                      (mstart (plist-get m :start))\n00383|                      (mend (plist-get m :end)))\n00384|                  (pcase mtype\n00385|                    ('file\n00386|                     (list :type \"file\"\n00387|                           :id (opencode-util--generate-id \"prt\")\n00388|                           :mime \"text/plain\"\n00389|                           :url (concat \"file://\" mpath)\n00390|                           :filename mname\n00391|                           :source (list :type \"file\"\n00392|                                         :text (list :value (substring text mstart mend)\n00393|                                                     :start mstart :end mend)\n00394|                                         :path mpath)))\n00395|                    ('agent\n00396|                     (list :type \"agent\"\n00397|                           :id (opencode-util--generate-id \"prt\")\n00398|                           :name mname\n00399|                           :source (list :value (substring text mstart mend)\n00400|                                         :start mstart :end mend))))))\n00401|              mentions)))\n00402|          (image-parts (when images\n00403|                         (mapcar\n00404|                          (lambda (img)\n00405|                            (list :type \"file\"\n00406|                                  :id (opencode-util--generate-id \"prt\")\n00407|                                  :mime (plist-get img :mime)\n00408|                                  :url (plist-get img :data-url)\n00409|                                  :filename Use 'offset' parameter to read beyond line 410)\n</file>","title":"opencode-api.el","metadata":{"preview":"(declare-function opencode-agent--find-by-name \"opencode-agent\" (name))\n(declare-function opencode-agent--primary-agents \"opencode-agent\" ())\n(declare-function opencode-agent--ensure-cache \"opencode-agent\" ())\n\n(defun opencode-api--fetch-agent-info ()\n  \"Populate model/provider state from /config.\nSets `opencode-api--model-id' and `opencode-api--provider-id'.\nAlso ensures the agent cache is populated for downstream consumers.\"\n  (opencode--debug \"opencode-api: fetching model info...\")\n  (condition-case err\n      (progn\n        (opencode-agent--ensure-cache)\n        (let* ((config (opencode-api-get-sync \"/config\"))\n               (model-str (when config (plist-get config :model))))\n          (when (and model-str (string-match \"\\\\`\\\\(.+\\\\)/\\\\(.+\\\\)\\\\'\" model-str))\n            (setq opencode-api--provider-id (match-string 1 model-str)\n                  opencode-api--model-id (match-string 2 model-str))))\n        (opencode--debug \"opencode-api: model=%s provider=%s\"\n                 opencode-api--model-id opencode-api--provider-id))\n    (error","truncated":true},"time":{"start":1772839917236,"end":1772839919004}}}}}}

:hidden) t)))\n00051|           (push agent result))))\n00052|     (nreverse result)))\n00053| \n00054| (defun opencode-agent--default-name ()\n00055|   \"Return the default agent name from cache.\n00056| Computes the first primary, non-hidden agent name on demand.\"\n00057|   (opencode-agent--ensure-cache)\n00058|   (when-let* ((primary (opencode-agent--primary-agents))\n00059|
